返回本批次

显示 HN：Littlebird – 屏幕阅读是人工智能中缺失的环节

Show HN: Littlebird – Screenreading is the missing link in AI

littlebird.ai 作者 delu 分数 41 评论 21 2026/03/24 01:39
打开 HN 原帖 打开原始文章

中文摘要

  1. Littlebird 如何理解我的作品？Littlebird 通过看到你所看到的来学习。它会关注屏幕上的活动窗口，例如您正在编写的文档或您正在浏览的网站。在会议期间，它会倾听并为您做笔记。它永远不会看到最小化的应用程序、私人浏览器窗口或密码等敏感信息。如果您在屏幕上看不到它，Littlebird 也看不到。 Littlebird 不会记录您的屏幕，而是读取活动应用程序的文本和元素。您始终处于掌控之中，可以随时暂停上下文收集或删除您的数据（所有数据，或只是最近的数据，例如最后一小时或一天）。我需要连接所有应用程序才能让 Littlebird 工作吗？不需要，这就是 Littlebird 如此强大的原因。它会通过查看屏幕上的内容自动工作，为您的所有应用程序提供统一的内存，无需任何设置。但是，我们正在引入与您喜爱的应用程序的可选集成。例如，您可以将 Littlebird 与 Google 日历集成，以帮助代表您安排会议。为什么我应该使用 Littlebird 而不是通用 AI 助手或聊天机器人？通用 AI 助手与您的工作无关。您必须通过复制和粘贴信息来赶上他们，这很慢并且会引发隐私问题。 Littlebird 已经了解您的请求背后的工作（您的文档、对话和项目），因此它可以为您提供更相关的答案和更强有力的起点。这使得 Littlebird 更快、更个性化，并且对于日常工作也更加有用。Littlebird 我的数据安全吗？是的。我们极其谨慎地对待您的数据，并使用一流的行业安全实践来保护它。您的信息在存储在我们的服务器上时始终会被加密，并且 Littlebird 与您授权的第三方服务之间的任何连接在传输过程中也会被加密。 ‍我们通过了 SOC 2 认证，这意味着我们的安全实践定期由独立第三方审核。我们还遵守 GDPR 和 CCPA 规定。您还可以随时删除您的数据（全部数据，或仅删除最近的数据，例如过去一小时或一天）。我的数据存储在哪里？为了提供可靠且高质量的服务，Littlebird 将您的数据安全地存储在托管于 Amazon Web Services (AWS) 的云中。虽然 Littlebird 在您的计算机上运行并且您可以控制它所看到的内容，但它捕获的数据会被加密并存储在云端。您是否使用我的数据来训练人工智能模型或将其出售给第三方？不。我们不会使用您的个人数据来训练我们的人工智能模型，我们也绝不会将您的数据出售给广告商或任何其他第三方。我们的承诺是针对我们的用户，而不是针对广告商。Littlebird 可在哪些平台或设备上使用？Littlebird 目前可用于 Mac，很快就会支持其他平台。加入 Windows 等待名单，以便在 Windows 版 Littlebird 发布时收到通知。如果您在离开办公桌时需要向 Littlebird 提问，我们还提供 iOS 和 Android 版配套应用程序可供下载。

Original Summary

  1. How does Littlebird understand my work?Littlebird learns by seeing what you see. It pays attention to the active window on your screen—like the document you're writing or the website you're browsing. During meetings, it listens along to take notes for you.‍It never sees minimized apps, private browser windows, or sensitive information like passwords. If you can't see it on your screen, Littlebird can't either. Instead of recording your screen, Littlebird reads the text and elements of your active applications. You're always in control and can pause context collection at any time or delete your data (all of it, or just recent data like the last hour or day).Do I need to connect all my apps for Littlebird to work?No, and that's what makes Littlebird so powerful. It works automatically by seeing what's on your screen, giving you a unified memory across all your apps without any setup. However, we're introducing optional integrations with your favorite apps. For example, you can integrate Littlebird with Google Calendar to help schedule meetings on your behalf.Why should I use Littlebird instead of a general AI assistant or chatbot?General AI assistants have no context about your work. You have to catch them up by copying and pasting information, which is slow and raises privacy concerns. Littlebird already understands the work behind your requests—your documents, conversations, and projects—so it gives you more relevant answers and stronger starting points. This makes Littlebird faster, more personal, and far more useful for day-to-day work.Is my data secure with Littlebird?Yes. We treat your data with the utmost care and protect it using best-in-class industry security practices. Your information is always encrypted when it's stored on our servers, and any connection between Littlebird and third-party services you authorize is also encrypted in transit. ‍We're SOC 2 certified, which means our security practices are regularly audited by an independent third party. We're also GDPR and CCPA compliant. You can also delete your data at any time (all of it, or just recent data like the last hour or day).Where is my data stored?To provide a reliable and high-quality service, Littlebird securely stores your data in the cloud, hosted on Amazon Web Services (AWS). While Littlebird runs on your computer and you control what it sees, the data it captures is encrypted and stored in the cloud.Do you use my data to train AI models or sell it to third parties?No. We don’t use your personal data to train our AI models, and we will never sell your data to advertisers or any other third parties. Our commitment is to our users, not to advertisers.What platforms or devices is Littlebird available on?Littlebird is currently available for Mac, with support for other platforms coming soon. Join the Windows Waitlist to be notified when Littlebird for Windows has been released. We also have a companion app available for download on both iOS and Android, if you need to ask Littlebird questions when you’re away from your desk.

评论（默认中文）

munio 2026/03/24 15:17
作为一种分发策略，屏幕阅读方法确实很聪明——无需维护集成，无需破坏 OAuth 流程，第一天就可以解决所有问题。困难的部分不是技术，而是信任问题。倒带、无限，现在这一切都遇到了同样的问题：最有可能从中受益的人（工作流程复杂的忙碌的专业人士）恰恰是如果出现问题最容易受到影响的人。在出现可靠的本地优先路径之前，TAM 将保持较小规模。
The screenreading approach is genuinely clever as a distribution strategy — no integrations to maintain, no OAuth flows to break, works on day one with everything. The hard part isn't the tech though, it's the trust problem. Rewind, Limitless, and now this all hit the same wall: the people most likely to benefit from this (busy professionals with complex workflows) are exactly the people most exposed if something goes wrong. Until there's a credible local-first path, the TAM is going to stay small.
在 HN 中查看
divmain 2026/03/24 04:25
您将来是否有可能支持本地优先版本？我一直对这样的应用程序很感兴趣，尤其是 Littlebird 似乎非常有吸引力。但我讨厌将我所有活动的屏幕截图/摘要/等发送到云解决方案，无论您对加密做出任何声明。你犯的任何错误对我来说都可能是灾难性的，这完全决定了使用你的产品的任何好处。这是不可能的。
Is there any chance you might support a local-first version of this in the future? I've been interested in apps like this and Littlebird in particular seems very attractive. But I'm loathe to essentially send screenshots/summaries/etc of all my activity to a cloud solution, regardless of any claims you make about encryption. Any mistake you make could be catastrophic for me, which thoroughly dominates any upside to using your product. It's a non-starter.
在 HN 中查看
grena1re 2026/03/24 04:53
我们肯定会的，但问题是，如果没有本地法学硕士，就无法提供真正完全本地化的版本。当地的法学硕士很愚蠢。所以基本上，你仍然需要信任法学硕士提供商。 完全理解这对于某些人来说是一个交易破坏者，但对于许多用户来说，理论上的风险是值得的。我们定期进行安全审核、传输中和静态加密、渗透测试等。
We will for sure, but the issue is that without local LLMs, there's no way to offer a truly fully local version. And the local LLMs are dumb. So basically, you would still need to trust the LLM providers. Totally understand that this is a deal breaker for some people, but for many users, the theoretical risk is worth it. We do regular security audits, encrypt in transit and at rest, pen tests, etc.
在 HN 中查看
throwaway-blaze 2026/03/24 06:14
嗯，以“当地的法学硕士很愚蠢”为由而忽视这项技术似乎是短视的。我可以在本地 Mac 上运行一些令人印象深刻的模型，但它有 >64GB 的内存和 M3 Max。

考虑到隐私方面的好处，我不会这么快就拒绝他们。 我建议选择一两个你的提示可以很好地配合的，并将其视为“如果你有一台能够运行本地模型的计算机，我们也允许你运行本地模型”。 这将（a）让那些抱怨一切的人安静下来，（b）让更多的人尝试云模型，因为他们知道他们可以转移到本地模型进行实际使用。

Um, dismissing the tech as "the local LLMs are dumb" seems shortsighted. I can run some pretty impressive models on my local Mac, but it has >64gb of ram and an M3 Max.

Given the privacy benefit I wouldn't dismiss them so fast. I'd suggest picking one or two that your prompts will work well with and treating it as "we let you run with local models too, if you have a computer capable of that." This will (a) quiet the people who complain about everything and (b) get more people to try the cloud model knowing they could move to a local model for real usage.

在 HN 中查看
grena1re 2026/03/24 07:42
我不会解雇他们。我是说他们还没到那儿。作为一家初创公司，我们必须优先考虑。我们无法同时完成所有工作，并且拥有双架构以及潜在的更多安全漏洞将是一项巨大的工程工作。 想要开办本地法学硕士的人非常少。我在飞行时使用当地的法学硕士，这是我个人的评估。它们都是基准最大化的，无法可靠的工具调用或在有意义的长时间对话中保持一致性。
I'm not dismissing them. I'm saying they're not there yet. As a startup, we have to prioritize. We can't do everything simultaneously, and it would be a substantial engineering effort to have a dual architecture as well as potentially more security holes. And the amount of people that want to run local LLMs is very small. I use local LLMs when I'm on flights, and that is my personal assessment. They are all benchmark-maxed and incapable of reliable tool calling or consistency over meaningfully long conversations.
在 HN 中查看
reasonmethis 2026/03/24 05:21
嘿，我是来自 Littlebird 的德米特里。对于您的用例，您是否希望底层 LLM 也位于本地，以便您的数据不会发送到“大狗”LLM 提供商？这是一个重要的考虑因素，因为那时它就不会那么聪明了——尽管我完全理解这是否是它对你有用的唯一方法。

顺便说一句，我们符合 SOC2、GDPR 和 CCPA 标准。

Hey, I'm Dmitriy from Littlebird. For your use case, would you want the underlying LLM to be local as well, so that your data doesn't get sent to the "big dog" LLM providers? It's an important consideration because it wouldn't be nearly as smart then - though I totally understand if that's the only way it could work for you.

We are SOC2, GDPR, and CCPA compliant btw.

在 HN 中查看
timwis 2026/03/24 06:23
我不是OP，但我来这里是为了表达同样的担忧。我会 爱 使用这样的东西。我还注册了 rewind.ai 和 Limitless 并预订了吊坠。但最终出于隐私考虑我取消了它。

我想知道它是否可以是本地存储，并且您可以提供自己的开放路由器端点吗？这样，它可以是本地模型，也可以是您自己在 Azure/Bedrock/Vertex 等中部署的 GPT/Claude，您可以在其中控制保留策略等。

基本上，我想知道你们无权查看我的资料。我知道这限制了您改进产品和支持问题的能力，但是当我发送 一切 这真的开始变得重要了。只是想分享一下是什么阻碍了我立即注册，尽管我真的很想使用这样的产品！

I'm not the OP but I came here to voice the same concern. I would love to use something like this. I also signed up for rewind.ai and Limitless and pre-ordered the pendant. But ultimately I cancelled it out of privacy concerns.

I wonder if it could be local storage and you could provide your own Open Router endpoint? That way it could be a local model or your own deployment of GPT/Claude in Azure/Bedrock/Vertex etc where you can control retention policies etc.

Basically, I want to know that you guys don't have access to view my stuff. I get that that limits your ability to improve the product and support issues, but when I'm sending everything it really starts to matter. Just thought I'd share what held me back from immediately signing up despite really wanting to use a product like this!

ismail 2026/03/24 07:25
同样在这里。虽然这看起来很有用，但当我看到所有数据都发送到云端时，它就成为了阻碍。

我有另一个应用程序可以跟踪我在应用程序中的所有使用情况（时间）。窗位，但我购买了它，因为它可以在本地保存。

Same here. While this looks like it would be beneficial , soon as I saw that all data gets sent to the cloud, It was a blocker.

I have another app that tracks all my usage (time ) at an application. Window level, but I purchased it because it saves locally.

这不是很像微软的 Windows 11 召回功能吗？
Isn't this a lot like Microsoft's Windows 11 Recall feature that they got a lot of flak for?
在 HN 中查看
grena1re 2026/03/24 01:47
Littlebird 是一款桌面应用程序，它会记住您所做的一切。 会议、消息、文档、浏览等。它可以帮助您保持专注、确定优先级、回忆并推动项目向前发展。与当今市场上的任何产品不同，Littlebird 使用屏幕阅读来理解屏幕上的所有文本，适用于所有应用程序，无需任何繁琐的设置。 它了解谁在何时说什么，并详细跟踪您的项目。它利用这种背景来建立对你生活的丰富理解：谁对你重要，你正在做什么，以及本周和今年你关心什么。它可以扩展您的工作记忆以及思考和创造的能力。

您可以控制 Littlebird 看到什么、记住什么以及忘记什么。默认情况下，我们将 Littlebird 设计为私密、安全且由用户控制。

Littlebird is a desktop app that remembers everything you’ve been working on. Meetings, messages, docs, browsing, etc. It helps you stay focused, prioritize, recall, and move projects forward. Unlike any product on the market today, Littlebird uses screenreading to understand all the text on screen, for all applications, without any cumbersome setup. It understands who said what, when, and keeps track of your projects in great detail. It uses that context to build a rich understanding of your life: who matters to you, what you're working on, and what you care about this week and this year. It extends your working memory and your capacity to think and create.

You control what Littlebird sees, what it remembers, and what it forgets. We designed Littlebird to be private, secure, and user-controlled by default.

在 HN 中查看
rsingel 2026/03/24 04:22
如果您认为 Slack 日志在发现方面是很糟糕的，请等到起诉或起诉您的人发现您输入和查看的所有内容等都在云中
If you thought Slack logs were damning in discovery, wait til someone suing or prosecuting you figures out that everything you typed and looked at, etc., is in the cloud
在 HN 中查看
Paulo75 2026/03/24 07:12
屏幕阅读是解决集成问题的明智方法。此空间中的所有其他工具都可以让您将每个应用程序一一连接，并且您始终等待它们支持您的工作流程。这只是看你看什么。事后看来很明显 - 很酷的东西
Screenreading is a smart way to solve the integration problem. Every other tool in this space makes you connect each app one by one and you're always waiting for them to support your workflow. This just watches what you watch. Feels obvious in hindsight - cool stuff
在 HN 中查看
steve-atx-7600 2026/03/24 10:35
我希望克劳德·科沃克能在这方面做得更好。我经常让 Claude 执行涉及多个窗口的临时任务，而且速度非常慢。 例如，在输入一个 Google 数据表单元格后，它会停止截屏并思考截屏以进行确认。 我相信随着时间的推移它会变得更好。
I wish Claude cowork could get better at this. I often end up with Claude performing ad hoc tasks involving multiple windows and it’s so slow. It stops and screenshots and thinks and screenshots to confirm after entering one Google sheet cell of data for example. I’m sure it’ll get better over time.

Looks cool! Reminds me of Rewind but better. (edit: for completion)

tried this last week. typed three words, got back a full breakdown that pulled from three different tools i'd had open. didn't brief it once.

Good tool. Any plans for Windows ? It's my main workstation.
